content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch against 808707cede105036103429ccad825cf9faddd8f6 and found two fix-now issues: controller recovery now rejects a valid all-terminal same-bead history, and the new create-time duplicate scan can falsely block on unrelated manual projects that only share the default bead-derived id.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: `find_existing_bead_project` now treats an all-terminal same-bead history as fatal ambiguity whenever more than one completed project matches (src/cli/project.rs:909-921), even though the create path explicitly allows that state (src/cli/project.rs:640-645). Because `ensure_project_for_controller` calls this helper before it can create or adopt anything (src/cli/milestone.rs:1146-1158), `milestone run` can no longer recover after a restart/controller desync when a bead has a completed default project plus a completed retry; the command fails instead of selecting a recoverable project or creating a fresh one. affected_files: src/cli/project.rs, src/cli/milestone.rs. remediation_hint: keep all-terminal same-bead histories recoverable here too by preferring a deterministic completed candidate (for example the default or newest completed project) or by falling through to fresh creation instead of raising ambiguity.
   Rationale: This is a regression in the controller recovery path, not just a message issue. The branch explicitly permits multiple terminal projects for the same bead, but the new recovery logic turns that valid history into an unrecoverable `milestone run` failure whenever `active_task_id` is missing.
2. finding_summary: The new create-time duplicate guard reuses `matching_bead_project_ids`, whose legacy fallback treats any task-source-free project whose id equals the bead’s default generated id as a same-bead match (src/cli/project.rs:759-805). `reject_existing_same_bead_project_creation` then blocks `task create` based on that inferred match (src/cli/project.rs:623-690), so an unrelated manual project named like the default bead id is now misclassified as an existing bead task and the operator is told to start/resume it instead of creating the requested project. affected_files: src/cli/project.rs. remediation_hint: restrict the default-id/no-task-source fallback to explicit recovery paths for legacy bead-backed projects, or require actual task-source/lineage evidence before the create-time blocker fires.
   Rationale: This extends a heuristic that was previously only used for recovery into the hard pre-claim duplicate check. The result is a false-positive rejection of legitimate `task create` requests whenever a manually created project happens to use the default bead-derived id.
